definitions.technical: The provision in GENIUS Act Section 17(f) that removes compliant stablecoins from the Commodity Exchange Act's definition of 'commodity.' If a stablecoin issuer loses its permitted status, this exclusion lapses, potentially shifting regulatory jurisdiction from the OCC to the CFTC.
definitions.lay: A section of the GENIUS Act that says compliant stablecoins are not commodities. This matters because if a stablecoin company stops following the rules, its tokens could suddenly be classified as commodities, bringing in a completely different set of regulators.
definitions.parenthetical: (the GENIUS Act provision excluding compliant stablecoins from the commodity definition, contingent on permitted status)
definitions.legal_significance: Section 17(f) creates a regulatory cliff: loss of permitted status simultaneously removes the commodity exclusion, potentially triggering CFTC jurisdiction at the worst possible time (during a crisis). The comment letter argues this gap requires a formal OCC/CFTC coordination agreement.
